Theory: if no second apellido, mark review; always keep raw; do not invent.
Theory: str methods first for literal cleanup before regex.

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Theory/demo: mid Jaccard → review with evidence; never auto-merge or assert kinship.

exercises.code:
import unicodedata
names = ['José', 'Jose\u0301', '']
for s in names:
    print(repr(s), '→', repr(unicodedata.normalize('NFC', s)))

a, b = 'MAÑANA', 'mañana'
match = a.casefold() == b.casefold()
print(match)

import unicodedata
a = 'José'
b = 'Jose\u0301'
print('raw equal', a == b)
na, nb = unicodedata.normalize('NFC', a), unicodedata.normalize('NFC', b)
print('NFC equal', na == nb)
print('causa: NFD residual / code points distintos hasta NFC')
exercises.justification_from_packet: Demo S07-T1-B raw False NFC True.

exercises.code:
raw = 'Ana María Quispe Huamán'
toks = raw.split()
ap1, ap2 = toks[-2:]
given = ' '.join(toks[:-2])
print(given)
print(ap1, ap2)

raw = 'María del Carmen Quispe Ríos'
toks = raw.split()
ap1, ap2 = toks[-2:]
given = ' '.join(toks[:-2])
print(given)
print(ap1, ap2)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Demo María del Carmen as given; particles preserved.
Theory: <3 tokens → review; keep raw; do not invent apellido2.

fields = [p.strip() for p in line.split(',')]
print(fields)

toks = ['Jr.', 'Unión', '450']

exercises.justification_from_packet: Theory replace/filter digits without regex first.

exercises.code:
raw = '(+51) 999-000-111'
digits = ''.join(ch for ch in raw if ch.isdigit())
print(digits)

import re
email = 'user+tag@example.com'
pat = r'^[a-z]+@[a-z]+\.com$'
rejected = re.fullmatch(pat, email) is None
print('rejected', rejected)
print('política: check modesto de @ (strip+lower); no regex overfit')
exercises.justification_from_packet: Demo overfit rejects plus addressing; prefer modest @ check.

exercises.code:
import re
pat = r'^[A-Z]{3}$'
print(bool(re.fullmatch(pat, 'LIM')))
print(bool(re.fullmatch(pat, 'Lima')))

import re
pat = re.compile(r'^(?P<nom>\w+) (?P<ap>\w+)$')
m = pat.fullmatch('Ana Quispe')
print(m.groupdict() if m else None)

import re
text = 'DNI 12345678'
print('search', bool(re.search(r'\d{8}', text)))
print('fullmatch', bool(re.fullmatch(r'\d{8}', text)))
print('nota: fullmatch para validar campo exacto; search para extraer en texto')
exercises.justification_from_packet: Demo search vs fullmatch on DNI text.

exercises.code:
import re
phone = re.compile(r'\b9\d{8}\b')
for s in ['ok 999111222', 'sin match 123']:
    print(s, phone.findall(s))

import re
log = 'ok LIM-01 y CUS-02 fin'
codes = re.findall(r'[A-Z]{3}-\d{2}', log)
print(codes)

print("patrón peligroso: (a+)+b sobre strings largos de a's")
print('riesgo: catastrophic backtracking con cuantificadores anidados ambiguos')
print('mitigación: patrones simples, a+b, o str methods; evita overfit')
exercises.justification_from_packet: Theory catastrophic backtracking; prefer simple patterns.
Demo decision review for mid scores; no auto-merge.
